calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2026 Continued To Aug-12-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ZIHUI TONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Tong's motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is DENIED.

The court liberally construes the complaint per Code of Civil Procedure section 452. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts showing malice, oppression, or fraud to support the claim for punitive damages. (Civil Code, sec. 3294, subd. (a).)

"[M]alice does not require actual intent to harm. [Citation.] Conscious disregard for the safety of another may be sufficient where the defendant is aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his or her conduct and he or she willfully fails to avoid such consequences. [Citation]." (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299.)

Here, plaintiff's ultimate facts sufficiently allege defendant's despicable and illegal conduct. (Complaint, Ex. A, p. 5.) In Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, the court found the punitive damages allegations sufficient because the intoxicated driver was speeding over the posted speed limit and driving erratically. Here, plaintiff similarly pleads facts showing despicable conduct. Not only was defendant intoxicated and deliberately chose to drive, he also knowingly accepted plaintiff as a passenger while acting as a commercial driver.

The court further notes that defendant failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 435.5, subdivision (a). Defendant did not meet and confer with plaintiff "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" prior to filing this motion. (Id.; Knaack Decl., par. 4). A bare letter is insufficient. The court exercises its discretion to reach the merits of the motion. The court also exercises its discretion to accept and consider the late-served opposition brief. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
